classes.name: (Af) Tropical rainforest climate
classes.description: Hot, humid, and rainy year-round—think the Amazon Basin, Congo Basin, or coastal Indonesia where seasons barely change.

classes.name: (Am) Tropical monsoon climate
classes.description: Steam-bath humidity with an intense wet season and a short drier break, like Mumbai, coastal Thailand, or Guyana.

classes.name: (Aw) Tropical savannah climate
classes.description: Warm year-round with distinct wet and dry periods across East Africa, the Venezuelan Llanos, and northern Australia.

classes.name: (BWh) Hot desert climate
classes.description: Scorching days, minimal rain, and clear skies typify the Sahara, Arabian Peninsula, and central Australian deserts.

classes.name: (BWk) Cold desert climate
classes.description: Dry like a desert but with chilly winters, as in the Gobi, Patagonian interior, or Colorado Plateau.

classes.name: (BSh) Hot semi-arid climate
classes.description: Transitional steppe zones bordering hot deserts such as the Sahel, Sudan, and northern Mexico.

classes.name: (BSk) Cold semi-arid climate
classes.description: Wide temperature swings with modest rainfall across Mongolia, Kazakhstan, and the U.S. High Plains.

classes.name: (Csa) Mediterranean with hot summers
classes.description: Mild, wet winters and hot, dry summers like coastal California, southern Spain, and central Chile.

classes.name: (Csb) Mediterranean with warm summers
classes.description: Gentler summers and rainy winters resembling coastal Portugal or the Pacific Northwest.

classes.name: (Csc) Mediterranean with cool summers
classes.description: Rare cool-summer Mediterranean pockets in high elevations of coastal Chile or western Turkey.

classes.name: (Cwa) Temperate with dry winters, hot summers
classes.description: Sticky summers and dry winters common in southeastern China, northern India, and inland Brazil.

classes.name: (Cwb) Temperate with dry winters, warm summers
classes.description: Balanced warmth with winter dryness across Ethiopian highlands and the Bolivian Yungas.

classes.name: (Cwc) Temperate with dry winters, cool summers
classes.description: Mild summers but distinct dry winters in the higher Andes valleys and some Himalayan foothills.

classes.name: (Cfa) Humid subtropical with hot summers
classes.description: Soupy summers and no true dry season like the U.S. Southeast, eastern China, and coastal Argentina.

classes.name: (Cfb) Humid subtropical with warm summers
classes.description: Gentler four-season climate similar to Paris Basin, southern Brazil, or New Zealand’s North Island.

classes.name: (Cfc) Humid oceanic with cool summers
classes.description: Persistent drizzle, cool summers, and mild winters like Scotland, southern Chile, or New Zealand’s South Island.

classes.name: (Dsa) Continental with dry summers, hot summers
classes.description: Large seasonal swings with dry summers and hot peaks across interior Turkey and Iran’s uplands.

classes.name: (Dsb) Continental with dry summers, warm summers
classes.description: Moderately warm summers yet drying trends, as in the Balkans or interior Pacific Northwest.

classes.name: (Dsc) Continental with dry summers, cool summers
classes.description: Short, cool summers and dry seasons in subalpine Mongolia or Siberia.

classes.name: (Dsd) Continental with dry summers, severe winters
classes.description: Subarctic-style winters paired with dry summers, typical of northern Siberia or interior Alaska.

classes.name: (Dwa) Continental with dry winters, hot summers
classes.description: Bitterly cold winters yet sultry summers across northeastern China, Manchuria, and northern Korea.

classes.name: (Dwb) Continental with dry winters, warm summers
classes.description: Similar regime with milder summers throughout highland Nepal, northern Japan, and interior Canada.

classes.name: (Dwc) Continental with dry winters, cool summers
classes.description: Short cool summers and frigid, dry winters like central Siberia and northern Mongolia.

classes.name: (Dwd) Continental with dry winters, severe winters
classes.description: Among the harshest climates: extremely cold, dry winters near Yakutsk and the Lena River basin.

classes.name: (Dfa) Humid continental with hot summers
classes.description: Hot, stormy summers and snowy winters found in the U.S. Midwest, southern Ontario, and Ukraine.

classes.name: (Dfb) Humid continental with warm summers
classes.description: Classic four-season climates like New England, southern Scandinavia, and northern Japan.

classes.name: (Dfc) Humid continental with cool summers
classes.description: Cool summers and long winters dominating northern Canada, interior Alaska, and central Siberia.

classes.name: (Dfd) Humid continental with severe winters
classes.description: Extreme winters with short summers typical of northeastern Siberia and the Taymyr Peninsula.

classes.name: (ET) Polar tundra climate
classes.description: Permafrost landscapes with brief chilly summers, as in Arctic Canada and northern Alaska.

classes.name: (EF) Polar frost climate
classes.description: Perennial ice and snow with virtually no melt season, like central Antarctica or Greenland’s ice cap.
